summary: First full test iteration of ApexTrader Pro after the FastAPI refactor. Backend suite (pytest) covering health, market data, AI analysis (quant+LLM), demo paper trading, and frontend HTML pages — all 19 tests passing (100%). Frontend /analysis works well (bias hero, AI Trade Decision, Fear&Greed, deep-analysis LLM button, chatbot LLM reply all functional). Two significant frontend issues found: (1) /charts page hangs on 'INITIALIZING APEXTRADER PRO…' because the client-side Binance WebSocket is geo-blocked (HTTP 451) from the preview infrastructure, and there is no REST fallback for chart bootstrap; (2) the Strategy Backtester panel triggered by #btnRunBt is rendered inside a container with offsetHeight=0 on the 'Performance & Backtest' tab, so its detailed metrics (Sharpe, Expectancy R, Fees, Avg Win/Loss) never appear — only the auto-run EMA-Cross summary card (Win Rate / Profit Factor / Trades / Max Drawdown) is visible.

minor.issue: Response uses PascalCase 'Data' key (from cryptocompare-style feed) instead of snake_case; not a bug but inconsistent with rest of API (JS consumers must handle both). Test was updated to accept 'Data'.

ui_bugs.issue: Page is stuck on 'INITIALIZING APEXTRADER PRO...' loading overlay. mainCanvas remains blank (nonBlackPixels=0, natural size still 300x150). Header shows 'Connecting…'. Root cause: wss://stream.binance.com:9443/ws returns HTTP 451 from Kubernetes egress; the frontend chart engine appears to gate initial render on the WebSocket handshake with no REST bootstrap fallback, even though /api/candles works fine.

Detailed strategy backtest UI (button id=btnRunBt) is present in DOM (display:block, visibility:visible) but offsetHeight=0 — its ancestor tab panel is not being made visible when the tab is activated. Clicking btnRunBt via JS does not populate #btResults. Users only see the auto-generated EMA-Cross summary card (Win Rate 42.9% / PF 1.42 / Trades 14 / Max DD -11.7%), never Sharpe / Expectancy(R) / Avg Win-Loss / Fees which the code (analysis.js lines 2607-2757) is designed to render.

Loading overlay element is never fully hidden (getComputedStyle display=flex, visibility=visible, opacity=1) even after data is rendered underneath. On /analysis the underlying page is fully usable so this is cosmetic; on /charts the same behavior blocks the entire chart.

integration_issues.issue: Client depends on direct browser->Binance WebSocket. In the preview environment browsers can hit Binance (429 or 451 responses seen), which means live prices and candles never populate. Recommend: (a) proxy Binance WebSocket through backend at /api/ws/binance, or (b) bootstrap from /api/candles REST + poll /api/ticker on interval when WS fails.

action_items: Fix /charts page hang: add REST fallback (use /api/candles + /api/ticker polling) so chart renders even when browser->Binance WebSocket is blocked. Also hide #loadingOverlay after first successful data batch instead of gating on WS 'open' event.

Fix Performance & Backtest tab: ensure the tab-pane containing #btnRunBt / #btResults gets display:block (not just visibility) when the tab is activated. Currently the button has offsetHeight=0, so users never reach the detailed Sharpe/Expectancy-R/Fees metrics implemented in runSingleBacktest().
Auto-hide the #loadingOverlay on /analysis once bias hero card populates (currently stays visible but does not block interaction).
Optional: normalize /api/news response shape — either wrap upstream 'Data' into 'items' server-side, or document the shape in the API contract.
critical_code_review_comments: backend/server.py: chart data fetched via requests in threads is fine, but consider adding a simple in-memory TTL cache for /api/candles (30s) and /api/ticker (5s) to protect against Binance rate limits — right now every call to /api/ai/analysis (which fetches 6 timeframes) hits Binance freshly on cold cache and can be 30-40s.
backend/server.py uses deprecated FastAPI @app.on_event('startup'); should migrate to lifespan context. Non-blocking but throws DeprecationWarning.

backend/services/demo_trading.py uses SQLite (file persisted in repo at repositories/paper_trading.db). Fine for demo, but that DB file being committed can cause noisy diffs. Consider .gitignoring it.

frontend/analysis.js is 2700+ lines — should be split (backtest engine, chat, deep-analysis, charts) into ES modules per your existing convention. Violates the >700-line rule from the code review guidelines.
Frontend chart bootstrap should not hard-block on Binance WS; add graceful degradation.

No data-testid on chatInput, chatSend, run-backtest button, results panel. Only deep-analysis-btn/content have testids. Add data-testid to at least: chat input/send, backtest run/results, bias-hero, ai-trade-decision, fear-greed-gauge for future regression coverage.

success_rate.frontend: ~70% (analysis mostly OK, charts fully broken, backtest details unreachable)

test_credentials: None required — app runs in guest mode (no auth). /app/memory/test_credentials.md not present.
seed_data_creation: None. Demo paper-trading tests reset via /api/demo/reset?balance=10000 before each lifecycle test and clean up by closing opened position.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend suite lives at /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py — 19 tests, run with: pytest /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py -v --junitxml=/app/test_reports/pytest/pytest_results.xml. AI-analysis test can take ~30-40s cold, deep-analysis ~15-30s (LLM). For frontend, use REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL from /app/frontend/.env. Known infra caveat: browser and server-side calls to wss://stream.binance.com from this preview cluster return HTTP 451 — treat as environmental, but the frontend must still degrade gracefully (see action items). Selectors that DO exist and work: data-testid='deep-analysis-btn' / 'deep-analysis-content', #chatInput, Send button by text, #btnRunBt (in DOM but hidden by ancestor). Tab navigation on /analysis uses text-matched buttons (no testid): 'AI Command Center', 'Liquidity & Order Flow', 'Performance & Backtest', 'Risk & Chatbot', 'Demo Simulator'.
rca_of_the_issue: 1) /charts init hang RCA: charts/chart_engine.js gates the loading overlay dismissal on a successful Binance WebSocket open event. In this Kubernetes preview cluster wss://stream.binance.com:9443/ws returns HTTP 451 (regulatory geo-block on the container egress IP). Since there is no timeout+REST fallback, initialization never completes and #loadingOverlay stays display:flex forever. Reproduction: open /charts in browser → observe 'INITIALIZING APEXTRADER PRO…' persisting > 15s → DevTools shows 'WebSocket connection failed: Error during handshake: Unexpected response code: 451'. Mitigation: (a) if WS fails to open within N seconds, fall back to REST polling via /api/candles + /api/ticker; (b) always hide overlay once first candle batch is drawn regardless of WS state. 2) btnRunBt invisible RCA: the strategy-backtester block (analysis.html around #btnRunBt / #btResults) is nested inside a tab panel that only toggles the CSS class showing its siblings, but this specific inner block has additional container styles that leave offsetHeight=0. Reproduction: open /analysis → click 'Performance & Backtest' → scroll — the 'Run Strategy Backtest' button and its results panel are not rendered though other backtest widgets (EMA-Cross summary) are. Mitigation: audit the CSS visibility/height cascade for #btnRunBt's ancestor divs, and ensure the Performance tab-pane sets an explicit display:block/flex on all children.
